tasks.description: Section templates UI — Save as Template dialog on section panel, template browser dialog in canvas toolbar, deploy flow
acceptance_criteria: Save as Template button in section-detail-panel footer (next to Run as Checklist)
Clicking Save as Template opens Radix Dialog with name (pre-filled section name), description, and category inputs
Dialog submit calls createTemplate({ workspace_id, section_id, name, description, category }), shows toast on success, closes dialog
Templates button in canvas-view.tsx toolbar area (top-right, next to coloring button)
Clicking Templates opens Radix Dialog showing both DB templates and STARTER_TEMPLATES as cards
Each card shows: name, description, category badge, step count (from template_data.steps.length)
DB templates show delete button (trash icon); starters do not
Deploy button on each card — for DB templates: calls deployTemplate(id, { tab_id, position_x, position_y }); for starters: calls createTemplate first (saves to DB), then deployTemplate
position_x and position_y for deploy: use next available position (e.g., max existing section position_y + offset, or fixed fallback like 0,0)
After deploy success: refresh sections/steps/connections state, close dialog, show toast
Template browser dialog handles loading, empty, and error states

similar_files: src/components/canvas/coloring-panel.tsx — overlay panel pattern with CRUD
src/components/panels/section-detail-panel.tsx:329-338 — Run as Checklist button pattern in footer
patterns_to_follow: Radix Dialog for all modals (Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter from @/components/ui/dialog)
toastError() from @/lib/api/toast-helpers for error handling, toast() from sonner for success
Button variants: default (blue), secondary (gray), destructive (red) from @/components/ui/button
CVA styling: text-[12px], text-[var(--text-secondary)], bg-[var(--bg-surface)], border-[var(--border-subtle)]
Client API wrappers: fetchTemplates, createTemplate, deployTemplate, deleteTemplate already exist in client.ts
STARTER_TEMPLATES constant from src/lib/templates.ts — StarterTemplate interface (name, description, category, template_data)
Template type from database.ts — has id, workspace_id, name, description, category, template_data, created_by, created_at, updated_at
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly, no nested access
React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)
affected_urls: /w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] — canvas view with new Templates button + browser dialog

tasks.special_instructions: CRITICAL: canvas-view.tsx is 353 lines — keep additions compact. The template browser dialog can be inline in canvas-view.tsx (not a separate component file) — similar to how ColoringPanel is a separate component but the coloring button/toggle is inline. However, if the dialog exceeds ~80 lines, extract to a separate file like src/components/canvas/template-browser.tsx. For the Save as Template dialog on section-detail-panel.tsx: add a BookTemplate or Copy icon import from lucide-react, place Save as Template button ABOVE the Run as Checklist button in the footer. The deploy position calculation: use Math.max of existing section position_y values + 300 (or 100 if no sections exist) for position_y, and 100 for position_x. For deploying starters: call createTemplate with the starter's data (workspace_id from context, no section_id since it's a starter), get the returned template, then call deployTemplate with the new ID. After deploy, re-fetch sections/steps/connections by refreshing the page or adding items to local state from the deploy response. Simplest: router.refresh() after deploy to reload server data. The deploy response is { section_id: string } — after deploy, router.refresh() will pick up the new data.

tasks.description: Wrap StepNode and SectionNode custom React Flow node components in React.memo for performance
acceptance_criteria: StepNode component export wrapped in React.memo
SectionNode component export wrapped in React.memo
Both components still render correctly (no visual changes)

patterns_to_follow: React.memo wrapping pattern: export const StepNode = React.memo(function StepNode(...) { ... })
Or: const StepNodeInner = function StepNode(...) { ... }; export const StepNode = React.memo(StepNodeInner);
React Flow docs explicitly recommend React.memo on custom node components
affected_urls: /w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] — canvas view (performance improvement, no visual changes)

tasks.special_instructions: Simple wrap — rename existing export function to inner function, wrap with React.memo, export the memo'd version. Preserve existing function signature and component display name. step-node.tsx is 143 lines, section-node.tsx is 94 lines.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed this iteration. All client API wrappers and types already exist from FEAT-052 [1/2].

warnings_added: BUG-021 already fixed in current codebase (workspace-shell.tsx exclusion list has runbooks/activity/gap-analysis). Bug exists only on production which is behind by 19+ commits. Mark as resolved once deployed.
IMP-035 already resolved — sidebar.tsx has all 11 navigation links including Gap Analysis, Compare, Runbooks, Activity, and Dashboard.
